title: MailerLite vs ActiveCampaign (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare MailerLite vs ActiveCampaign: pricing, features, automation, and CRM capabilities to find the best email marketing tool for your needs.
intro: Both MailerLite and ActiveCampaign are powerful email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. MailerLite excels as an affordable, user-friendly solution for small businesses and freelancers, while ActiveCampaign offers advanced automation and integrated CRM for growing companies. This comparison helps you determine which platform best matches your budget and requirements.
choose_a_if: You're a small business or freelancer with a limited budget
You need a free tier to get started with email marketing
You want built-in landing pages and pop-ups for lead generation
E-commerce integrations and automation are your primary needs
choose_b_if: You need advanced automation with lead scoring and site tracking
You require integrated CRM functionality for sales pipeline management
You want SMS marketing capabilities in addition to email
You're a growing business ready to invest in a comprehensive platform

faq.a: Neither is universally "better"—it depends on your needs. MailerLite wins for affordability and ease of use, making it ideal for small businesses. ActiveCampaign is better for companies needing advanced automation, CRM, and lead scoring features. Choose based on your budget and feature requirements.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, MailerLite or ActiveCampaign?
faq.a: MailerLite is significantly cheaper, starting at $9/month with a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers. ActiveCampaign starts at $15/month with no free option. For budget-conscious businesses, MailerLite offers better value, though ActiveCampaign's pricing may be worthwhile for its advanced features.

faq.q: Can I switch from ActiveCampaign to MailerLite?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support data migration through CSV exports and integrations. However, some advanced automations or custom workflows may need to be recreated. It's recommended to plan your migration carefully and test your email sequences before fully switching to ensure continuity.
